completed_tasks.name: Async foundation: tokio-util, config, trait redesign, TelegramAdapter
Core wiring: polling, dispatch, user queue, handler, runner
End-to-end Telegram bot smoke test
completed_tasks.progress: Bot connects, registers commands, receives messages, dispatches to handler. Agent loop starts but LLM response never arrives in Telegram.

blockers.description: Agent loop starts but LLM response never appears in Telegram. Bot connects, polls, dispatches correctly. Handler sends placeholder, creates AgentLoop, calls agent.run() which logs 'Starting agent loop' but never completes.
blockers.workaround: Run with RUST_LOG=debug to get debug-level logs from client.rs which now has tracing around the HTTP request and response status. This will show whether the request hangs at connect, response, or stream parsing.

human_actions_pending.action: Run 'RUST_LOG=debug cargo run -- gateway' and send a message, share debug logs
human_actions_pending.context: Need to see where the LLM streaming request stalls

decisions.rationale: Cooperative shutdown propagates to all subtasks cleanly

decisions.decision: dispatch_future must run in tokio::select! with shutdown signal
decisions.rationale: Bug fix: was defined but only awaited after ctrl+c, so dispatch never ran

decisions.decision: Handler must use config.resolve_api_key() not config.model.api_key
decisions.rationale: Bug fix: env var resolution was bypassed, giving empty API key

next_action: Run with RUST_LOG=debug cargo run -- gateway, send a message, check debug logs for 'Sending streaming LLM request' and 'LLM response received' to identify where the streaming call stalls. The client.rs now has debug tracing at both points. If the request hangs before 'LLM response received', it's a connection/timeout issue. If it gets 200 but no stream data, it's an SSE parsing issue.
context_notes: All 5 plans code-complete. 26 gateway tests pass. Two bugs already fixed: (1) dispatch_future not running concurrently, (2) API key not resolved from env vars. Current bug is that agent.run() never completes — likely the streaming HTTP request to OpenRouter hangs or returns data that doesn't parse. Added connect_timeout(30s) and debug logging to client.rs but haven't tested yet.
